title: Fastest-Growing Federal Markets, FY2026
h1: Fastest-growing federal contract markets, FY2026 to date
metaDescription: The fastest-growing federal contract markets by same-period year-over-year growth in FY2026 to date, from USAspending.gov. Led by Commercial and Institutional

charts.takeaway: Commercial and Institutional Building Construction was the fastest-growing established federal market, up 226.3% versus the same period a year earlier.

narrative.intro: This ranking shows year-over-year growth in federal contract obligations for the FY2026 to date period. Total obligations across the markets shown were $160B for the fiscal year to date. The list highlights individual markets with the largest percentage increases in contract obligations compared with the same period in the prior year, led by Commercial and Institutional Building Construction at 226.3% and followed by Process, Physical Distribution, and Logistics Consulting Services at 174.0%. Other markets in the ranking include Highway, Street, and Bridge Construction at 154.6%, Automobile Manufacturing at 96.8%, Offices of Physicians (except Mental Health Specialists) at 73.1%, Research and Development in Biotechnology (except Nanobiotechnology) at 62.9%, Telecommunications Resellers at 59.9%, Electronic and Precision Equipment Repair and Maintenance at 51.9%, Software Publishers at 51.8%, and Guided Missile and Space Vehicle Manufacturing at 42.5%. All figures reported are contract obligations and current-year totals are partial (fiscal year to date). Use these growth rates to identify which NAICS-linked markets have seen the largest year-over-year increases in reported federal obligations in FY2026 to date.

sections.heading: Which markets showed the largest year-over-year increases?
sections.body: Commercial and Institutional Building Construction led the ranking with growth of 226.3%. Process, Physical Distribution, and Logistics Consulting Services recorded 174.0%, and Highway, Street, and Bridge Construction recorded 154.6%. Other notable increases include Automobile Manufacturing at 96.8% and Offices of Physicians (except Mental Health Specialists) at 73.1%.

sections.heading: What is the scope and nature of these growth figures?
sections.body: These figures are changes in contract obligations for the FY2026 to date period compared with the same period in the prior year. The values are reported as percentage increases in obligations and reflect fiscal year to date totals, which are partial for the current year. The ranking lists markets by their year-over-year percentage change in obligations.

faq.q: What are the fastest-growing federal contract markets?
faq.a: The fastest-growing federal contract markets are the markets listed in the ranking, led by Commercial and Institutional Building Construction at 226.3%, Process, Physical Distribution, and Logistics Consulting Services at 174.0%, and Highway, Street, and Bridge Construction at 154.6%, followed by Automobile Manufacturing at 96.8%, Offices of Physicians (except Mental Health Specialists) at 73.1%, Research and Development in Biotechnology (except Nanobiotechnology) at 62.9%, Telecommunications Resellers at 59.9%, Electronic and Precision Equipment Repair and Maintenance at 51.9%, Software Publishers at 51.8%, and Guided Missile and Space Vehicle Manufacturing at 42.5%.

faq.q: How is market growth measured here?
faq.a: Market growth here is measured as year-over-year changes in contract obligations for the same fiscal period; the figures are reported as percentage changes in obligations and current-year totals are partial (fiscal year to date).
